heading: Responsibilities for Federal information systems standards
label: 11331 Responsibilities for Federal information systems standards
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)1133140:1441.–(d), , .Pub. L. 104–106, div. E, title LI, § 5131(a)110 Stat. 687Feb. 10, 1996

Amendments2022— amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text related to the definition of “information security”, in subsec. (a); the requirement that the Director of the Office of Management and Budget promulgate information security standards, in subsec. (b); the application of more stringent standards by heads of agencies, in subsec. (c); and requirements regarding decisions by the Director, in subsec. (d).Pub. L. 117–1672002— amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text, as amended generally by , read as follows:Pub. L. 107–296Pub. L. 107–347“(a) .—Standards and Guidelines“(1) .—Except as provided under paragraph (2), the Secretary of Commerce shall, on the basis of standards and guidelines developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology pursuant to paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 20(a) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act (), prescribe standards and guidelines pertaining to Federal information systems.Authority to prescribe15 U.S.C. 278g–3(a)“(2) .—Standards and guidelines for national security systems (as defined under this section) shall be developed, prescribed, enforced, and overseen as otherwise authorized by law and as directed by the President.National security systems“(b) .—Mandatory Requirements“(1) .—Except as provided under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall make standards prescribed under subsection (a)(1) compulsory and binding to the extent determined necessary by the Secretary to improve the efficiency of operation or security of Federal information systems.Authority to make mandatory“(2) .—(A) Standards prescribed under subsection (a)(1) shall include information security standards that—Required mandatory standards“(i) provide minimum information security requirements as determined under section 20(b) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act (); and15 U.S.C. 278g–3(b)“(ii) are otherwise necessary to improve the security of Federal information and information systems.“(B) Information security standards described in subparagraph (A) shall be compulsory and binding.“(c) .—The President may disapprove or modify the standards and guidelines referred to in subsection (a)(1) if the President determines such action to be in the public interest. The President’s authority to disapprove or modify such standards and guidelines may not be delegated. Notice of such disapproval or modification shall be published promptly in the Federal Register. Upon receiving notice of such disapproval or modification, the Secretary of Commerce shall immediately rescind or modify such standards or guidelines as directed by the President.Authority to Disapprove or Modify“(d) .—To ensure fiscal and policy consistency, the Secretary shall exercise the authority conferred by this section subject to direction by the President and in coordination with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.Exercise of Authority“(e) .—The head of an executive agency may employ standards for the cost-effective information security for information systems within or under the supervision of that agency that are more stringent than the standards the Secretary prescribes under this section if the more stringent standards—Application of More Stringent Standards“(1) contain at least the applicable standards made compulsory and binding by the Secretary; and“(2) are otherwise consistent with policies and guidelines issued under .section 3543 of title 44“(f) .—The decision by the Secretary regarding the promulgation of any standard under this section shall occur not later than 6 months after the submission of the proposed standard to the Secretary by the National Institute of Standards and Technology, as provided under section 20 of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act ().Decisions on Promulgation of Standards15 U.S.C. 278g–3“(g) .—In this section:Definitions“(1) .—The term ‘Federal information system’ means an information system used or operated by an executive agency, by a contractor of an executive agency, or by another organization on behalf of an executive agency.Federal information system“(2) .—The term ‘information security’ has the meaning given that term in .Information securitysection 3542(b)(1) of title 44“(3) .—The term ‘national security system’ has the meaning given that term in .”National security systemsection 3542(b)(2) of title 44substituted “Responsibilities for Federal information systems standards” for “Responsibilities regarding efficiency, security, and privacy of federal computer systems” in section catchline and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 107–347“(a) .—In this section, the terms ‘federal computer system’ and ‘operator of a federal computer system’ have the meanings given those terms in section 20(d) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act ().Definitions15 U.S.C. 278g–3(d)“(b)Standards and Guidelines.—“(1)Authority to prescribe and disapprove or modify.—“(A) .—On the basis of standards and guidelines developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology pursuant to paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 20(a) of the Act (, (3)), the Secretary of Commerce shall prescribe standards and guidelines pertaining to federal computer systems. The Secretary shall make those standards compulsory and binding to the extent the Secretary determines necessary to improve the efficiency of operation or security and privacy of federal computer systems.Authority to prescribe15 U.S.C. 278g–3(a)(2)“(B) .—The President may disapprove or modify those standards and guidelines if the President determines that action to be in the public interest. The President’s authority to disapprove or modify those standards and guidelines may not be delegated. Notice of disapproval or modification shall be published promptly in the Federal Register. On receiving notice of disapproval or modification, the Secretary shall immediately rescind or modify those standards or guidelines as directed by the President.Authority to disapprove or modify“(2) .—To ensure fiscal and policy consistency, the Secretary shall exercise the authority conferred by this section subject to direction by the President and in coordination with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.Exercise of authority“(c) .—The head of a federal agency may employ standards for the cost-effective security and privacy of sensitive information in a federal computer system in or under the supervision of that agency that are more stringent than the standards the Secretary prescribes under this section if the more stringent standards contain at least the applicable standards the Secretary makes compulsory and binding.Application of More Stringent Standards“(d)Waiver of Standards.—“(1) .—The Secretary may waive in writing compulsory and binding standards under subsection (b) if the Secretary determines that compliance would—Authority of the secretary“(A) adversely affect the accomplishment of the mission of an operator of a federal computer system; or“(B) cause a major adverse financial impact on the operator that is not offset by Federal Government-wide savings.“(2) .—The Secretary may delegate to the head of one or more federal agencies authority to waive those standards to the extent the Secretary determines that action to be necessary and desirable to allow for timely and effective implementation of federal computer system standards. The head of the agency may redelegate that authority only to a chief information officer designated pursuant to .Delegation of waiver authoritysection 3506 of title 44“(3) .—Notice of each waiver and delegation shall be transmitted promptly to Congress and published promptly in the Federal Register.”Notice

Effective Date of 2002 AmendmentsAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under , Public Printing and Documents.Pub. L. 107–347section 402(b) of Pub. L. 107–347section 3504 of Title 44Dec. 17, 2002Amendment by effective 60 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under , Domestic Security.Pub. L. 107–296section 4 of Pub. L. 107–296section 101 of Title 6Nov. 25, 2002

children.heading: .—Authority to prescribe
children.label: 1 .—Authority to prescribe
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), the Secretary of Commerce shall, on the basis of standards and guidelines developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology pursuant to paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 20(a) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act (), prescribe standards and guidelines pertaining to Federal information systems.15 U.S.C. 278g–3(a)

children.heading: .—National security systems
children.label: 2 .—National security systems
children.text: Standards and guidelines for national security systems shall be developed, prescribed, enforced, and overseen as otherwise authorized by law and as directed by the President.

children.heading: .—Authority to make mandatory
children.label: 1 .—Authority to make mandatory
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), the Secretary of Commerce shall make standards prescribed under subsection (a)(1) compulsory and binding to the extent determined necessary by the Secretary to improve the efficiency of operation or security of Federal information systems.

children.label: i
children.text: provide minimum information security requirements as determined under section 20(b) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act (); and15 U.S.C. 278g–3(b)

children.label: ii
children.text: are otherwise necessary to improve the security of Federal information and information systems.

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: B .—Requirement
children.text: Information security standards described in subparagraph (A) shall be compulsory and binding.

children.heading: .—Authority to Disapprove or Modify
children.label: c .—Authority to Disapprove or Modify
children.text: The President may disapprove or modify the standards and guidelines referred to in subsection (a)(1) if the President determines such action to be in the public interest. The President’s authority to disapprove or modify such standards and guidelines may not be delegated. Notice of such disapproval or modification shall be published promptly in the Federal Register. Upon receiving notice of such disapproval or modification, the Secretary of Commerce shall immediately rescind or modify such standards or guidelines as directed by the President.

children.heading: .—Exercise of Authority
children.label: d .—Exercise of Authority
children.text: To ensure fiscal and policy consistency, the Secretary of Commerce shall exercise the authority conferred by this section subject to direction by the President and in coordination with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.
contain at least the applicable standards made compulsory and binding by the Secretary of Commerce; and
are otherwise consistent with policies and guidelines issued under .section 3553 of title 44

children.heading: .—Decisions on Promulgation of Standards
children.label: f .—Decisions on Promulgation of Standards
children.text: The decision by the Secretary of Commerce regarding the promulgation of any standard under this section shall occur not later than 6 months after the submission of the proposed standard to the Secretary by the National Institute of Standards and Technology, as provided under section 20 of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act ().15 U.S.C. 278g–3

children.heading: .—Federal information system
children.label: 1 .—Federal information system
children.text: The term “Federal information system” means an information system used or operated by an executive agency, by a contractor of an executive agency, or by another organization on behalf of an executive agency.

children.heading: .—Information security
children.label: 2 .—Information security
children.text: The term “information security” has the meaning given that term in .section 3552(b)(3) of title 44

children.heading: .—National security system
children.label: 3 .—National security system
children.text: The term “national security system” has the meaning given that term in .section 3552(b)(6) of title 44
